seoTitle: How to Create an Ebook With Google Docs and Canva
metaDescription: Draft your manuscript in Google Docs, then import it into Canva to design a polished ebook cover, layout, and imagery before exporting a print-ready PDF.
intro: Google Docs is great for drafting long-form text, but Canva is where an ebook actually gets its visual polish. This guide covers writing and structuring your manuscript in Docs, exporting it for migration, and then bringing it into Canva to add cover design, custom layouts, and imagery before generating your final print-ready file.

steps.heading: Draft your manuscript in Google Docs
steps.body: Write and organize your text using clean heading styles so your chapters are clearly structured before moving to design.

steps.heading: Export your text for migration
steps.body: Download your finished Google Docs manuscript as a .docx or .pdf file to prep it for import into Canva.

steps.heading: Choose an ebook template in Canva
steps.body: Switch to the Canva dashboard and browse eBook-specific templates to find a cohesive cover and page design that fits your content.

steps.heading: Import your text and visuals
steps.body: Bring your written content into Canva's text frames, sidebars, and callout boxes, then upload any supporting images or graphics.

steps.heading: Style typography and color themes
steps.body: Set consistent fonts, professional color palettes, and imagery placement to reinforce a cohesive visual identity throughout the ebook.

steps.heading: Export your finished ebook
steps.body: Use Canva's export dashboard to download your completed ebook as a high-quality print-ready PDF.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Canva plan to make an ebook?
faq.answer: Canva's free tier includes many templates and design tools suitable for ebooks, though some premium templates and elements require a paid plan.

faq.question: Can I edit the text again after moving it into Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, once imported, your text sits in editable frames within Canva, so you can revise wording directly inside the design.

faq.question: What file format should my Canva export use for distribution?
faq.answer: A print-ready PDF is generally the safest format for distributing your finished ebook across most platforms and readers.

faq.question: Is it better to write directly in Canva instead of Google Docs?
faq.answer: Drafting in Google Docs first is usually easier for long-form editing, since Canva is better suited to visual layout than heavy text revision.
